question.text: The Net Overnight Open Position Limit (NOOPL) for banks incorporated in India is set by their respective boards. What is the maximum limit as prescribed by RBI?

answer.text: 25% of total capital (Tier I and Tier II)
10% of total capital (Tier I and Tier II)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Per RBI guidelines, the NOOPL fixed by bank boards should not exceed 25% of the bank's total capital (Tier I and Tier II), or any other condition specified by RBI from time to time. **The Trap:** The limit applies to total capital (Tier I + Tier II), not just Tier I or net worth. **Cross-Exam Context:** Position limits and capital adequacy are tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For banks incorporated in India, NOOPL covers all branches including overseas branches and Offshore Banking Units. For foreign banks, it covers only Indian branches.
